# chronosphere_log_control_config (Resource)

Singleton org-wide pipeline of rules that sample, drop, replace, parse, or emit metrics from logs at ingest.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_log_control_config" "config" {
  # Sample 1% of debug-level logs from the sample service.
  rules {
    name   = "sample-debug"
    mode   = "ENABLED"
    filter = "service = 'sample-service' AND severity = 'debug'"
    type   = "SAMPLE"

    sample {
      rate = 0.01
    }
  }

  # Drop logs from a deprecated service entirely.
  rules {
    name   = "drop-deprecated"
    mode   = "ENABLED"
    filter = "service = 'deprecated-service'"
    type   = "DROP"
  }

  # Redact sensitive fields nested under kubernetes labels.
  rules {
    name   = "drop-sensitive-fields"
    mode   = "ENABLED"
    filter = "service = 'api-gateway'"
    type   = "DROP_FIELD"

    drop_field {
      field_regex = "password|secret|api_key"
      parent_path {
        selector = "kubernetes['labels']"
      }
    }
  }

  # Replace long trace IDs with a placeholder to reduce log volume.
  rules {
    name   = "shorten-trace-ids"
    mode   = "ENABLED"
    filter = "service = 'api-gateway'"
    type   = "REPLACE_FIELD"

    replace_field {
      field {
        selector = "trace_id"
      }
      replace_regex = "[0-9a-f]{32}"
      replace_all   = false
      replace_mode  = "STATIC_VALUE"
      static_value {
        value = "[trace-id]"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `rules` (Block List) Ordered list of log control rules applied to the log ingest pipeline. Rules are evaluated in order. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Optional:

- `drop_field` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the `DROP_FIELD` action, which removes fields from matching logs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--drop_field))
- `emit_metrics` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the `EMIT_METRICS` action, which derives Prometheus metrics from matching logs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics))
- `filter` (String) Log query filter that selects matching logs. The control action applies only to logs that match.
- `mode` (String) Execution mode for the rule (for example, `ENABLED` or `DISABLED`).
- `name` (String) User-defined name for the control rule.
- `parse_field` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the `PARSE_FIELD` action, which parses a field with a regex, key/value, or grok parser and writes the result to another field. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field))
- `replace_field` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the `REPLACE_FIELD` action, which rewrites field values in matching logs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--replace_field))
- `sample` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the `SAMPLE_LOGS` action, which keeps a fraction of matching logs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--sample))
- `type` (String) Type of control action this rule performs. Exactly one of the matching action blocks (`sample`, `drop_field`, `emit_metrics`, `replace_field`, `parse_field`) must be configured.

<a id="nestedblock--rules--drop_field"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.drop_field`

Optional:

- `field_regex` (String) Regular expression that selects which fields to drop.
- `parent_path` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--drop_field--parent_path))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--drop_field--parent_path"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.drop_field.parent_path`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics`

Optional:

- `counter` (Block List, Max: 1) Emit a counter metric. Exactly one of `counter`, `gauge`, or `histogram` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--counter))
- `drop_log` (Boolean) If `true`, drops the entire log after emitting the metric.
- `gauge` (Block List, Max: 1) Emit a gauge metric. Exactly one of `counter`, `gauge`, or `histogram` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--gauge))
- `histogram` (Block List, Max: 1) Emit a histogram metric. Exactly one of `counter`, `gauge`, or `histogram` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--histogram))
- `labels` (Block List) Labels to attach to the generated metric, specified as key/value pairs mapping a Prometheus label name to a log field path. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--labels))
- `mode` (String) Metric emission mode that controls how the metric is generated from matching logs.
- `name` (String) Name of the generated metric. Must conform to Prometheus naming conventions and be unique within the tenant.

<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--counter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.counter`

Optional:

- `value` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--counter--value))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--counter--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.counter.value`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--gauge"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.gauge`

Optional:

- `aggregation_type` (String) How multiple values are aggregated into the emitted gauge (for example, `LAST`, `MIN`, `MAX`).
- `value` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--gauge--value))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--gauge--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.gauge.value`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--histogram"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.histogram`

Optional:

- `value` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--histogram--value))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--histogram--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.histogram.value`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--labels"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.labels`

Optional:

- `key` (String) Prometheus label name to set on the emitted metric.
- `value` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--labels--value))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--emit_metrics--labels--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.emit_metrics.labels.value`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.




<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field`

Required:

- `parser` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Parser configuration. Exactly one of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set, matching `parser_type`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser))

Optional:

- `destination` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--destination))
- `source` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--source))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.parser`

Required:

- `parser_type` (String) Type of parser to apply. Determines which of `regex_parser`, `key_value_parser`, or `grok_parser` must be set.

Optional:

- `grok_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Grok parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `GROK`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--grok_parser))
- `key_value_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Key/value parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `KEY_VALUE`. Duplicate keys keep the first occurrence. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--key_value_parser))
- `regex_parser` (Block List, Max: 1) Regex parser configuration. Only set when `parser_type` is `REGEX`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--regex_parser))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--grok_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.parser.grok_parser`

Required:

- `pattern` (String) Grok pattern to apply. Named capture groups become named fields in the extracted log.


<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--key_value_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.parser.key_value_parser`

Required:

- `delimiter` (String) String used to split the input into individual key/value pairs.
- `pair_separator` (String) String used to split each pair into a key and value.

Optional:

- `trim_set` (String) Unicode code points to trim from the beginning and end of each key and value.


<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--parser--regex_parser"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.parser.regex_parser`

Required:

- `regex` (String) RE2 regular expression pattern. Named capturing groups become named fields in the extracted log.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.destination`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.


<a id="nestedblock--rules--parse_field--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.parse_field.source`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--replace_field"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.replace_field`

Optional:

- `field` (Block List, Max: 1) Path to a field within a log record. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--replace_field--field))
- `mapped_value` (Block List, Max: 1) Replace field values using a key/value lookup table. Exactly one of `mapped_value` or `static_value` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--replace_field--mapped_value))
- `replace_all` (Boolean) If `true`, replaces all matches. If `false`, replaces only the first match.
- `replace_mode` (String) Mode that controls how the replacement is applied to matched content.
- `replace_regex` (String) Regular expression that selects which part of the field value to replace.
- `static_value` (Block List, Max: 1) Replace matched content with a static string. Exactly one of `mapped_value` or `static_value` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--replace_field--static_value))

<a id="nestedblock--rules--replace_field--field"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.replace_field.field`

Optional:

- `selector` (String) Field path selector. Use `parent[child]` syntax to indicate nesting.


<a id="nestedblock--rules--replace_field--mapped_value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.replace_field.mapped_value`

Optional:

- `default_value` (String) Value to substitute when no matching key is found, when `use_default` is `true`.
- `pairs` (Block List) List of key/value pairs that map matched content to replacement values. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rules--replace_field--mapped_value--pairs))
- `use_default` (Boolean) If `true`, falls back to `default_value` when no key matches. If `false`, leaves the value unchanged on a miss.

<a id="nestedblock--rules--replace_field--mapped_value--pairs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.replace_field.mapped_value.pairs`

Optional:

- `key` (String) Matched content to look up.
- `value` (String) Replacement value to substitute when the key matches.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--replace_field--static_value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.replace_field.static_value`

Optional:

- `value` (String) Replacement value. If empty, the matched content is removed.



<a id="nestedblock--rules--sample"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.sample`

Optional:

- `rate` (Number) Fraction of matching logs to keep, in the range `[0, 1]` (for example, `0.25` keeps 25%).
